flashcards.front: What is the primary purpose of paging in memory management?
flashcards.back: Paging divides memory into fixed-size blocks to eliminate external fragmentation and enable non-contiguous allocation of processes.

flashcards.front: What causes internal fragmentation in memory systems?
flashcards.back: Internal fragmentation occurs when allocated memory blocks are larger than required, leaving unused space within pages.

flashcards.front: How does segmentation differ from paging?
flashcards.back: Segmentation divides memory into variable-sized logical units matching program structure, while paging uses fixed-size physical frames.

flashcards.front: What is the role of the MMU (Memory Management Unit)?
flashcards.back: MMU translates virtual addresses to physical addresses and manages memory protection through base/limit registers.
A phenomenon where increasing page frames leads to more page faults in FIFO page replacement algorithm.

flashcards.front: Define thrashing in virtual memory systems.
flashcards.back: Thrashing occurs when excessive page swapping degrades system performance due to insufficient physical memory.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of a TLB (Translation Lookaside Buffer)?
flashcards.back: TLB caches recent virtual-to-physical address translations to speed up memory access.

flashcards.front: How does demand paging optimize memory usage?
flashcards.back: Loads pages into memory only when needed, reducing initial memory footprint of processes.

flashcards.front: What is the working set model?
flashcards.back: A strategy that keeps actively used pages in memory to prevent thrashing by analyzing recent page references.

flashcards.front: Differentiate between logical and physical addresses.
flashcards.back: Logical addresses are generated by CPU (virtual), physical addresses are actual memory locations accessed via MMU translation.
Optimization where parent/child processes share pages until modification, reducing duplication overhead.

flashcards.front: How does the Optimal page replacement algorithm work?
flashcards.back: Replaces page that will not be used for longest future period (theoretical benchmark, not implementable).
